name: Chuck Faranda Professional Master Workflow
description: Second Edition publication workflow accompanying 'The Complete Siril & Seti Astro Suite Pro Processing Companion'. Designed for OSC and Mono data with emphasis on preserving signal, using AI tools judiciously, and validating each processing stage.

steps.note: Crop stacking artifacts. Preserve all valid astronomical signal. Inspect all four edges before proceeding.
Remove the pedestal without clipping the histogram. Verify the black point remains intact.
Preferred: AutoBGE. Alternatives: GraXpert AI, VeraLux Nox, Manual Background Extraction. Preserve IFN and faint dust; avoid over-correction.
Neutralize the background after gradient removal. Prefer SPCC/PCC workflow when appropriate. Verify the background remains free of color casts.
Perform photometric color calibration (SPCC preferred, PCC second). Confirm natural stellar colors before stretching.

Preferred sequence: BlurXTerminator (RC-Astro) → NoiseXTerminator (RC-Astro). Optional: StarXTerminator after denoising when beneficial. Alternatives: SyQon Parallax, SyQon Prism, Native tools. Inspect at 100% for halos, ringing, and loss of faint detail.
Preferred: Generalized Hyperbolic Stretch. Use Statistical Stretch for IFN and extremely faint dust. Apply multiple gentle stretches instead of one aggressive stretch.
Use conservative curves to refine contrast and color while preserving star color and faint structures. Compare frequently with the original.
Optional local contrast enhancement. Use sparingly to avoid exaggerated texture or noise.
Save a 16-bit TIFF master before producing JPEG or other delivery formats.

author: Chuck Faranda
book: The Complete Siril & Seti Astro Suite Pro Processing Companion
